text.title: TTS Implementation — Worked Example
text.summary: Worked synthesis of an 18000-character podcast episode through TTSService: 5 chunks, parallel synth, assembled mp3, cache + eviction.

example.code:
import asyncio, tempfile, hashlib, shutil, json from pathlib import Path from pydub import AudioSegment from openai import AsyncOpenAI
CACHE_ROOT = Path("out/audio/cache") client = AsyncOpenAI()
def _key(text, voice, speed, model, provider): return hashlib.sha256(f"{text}|{voice}|{speed}|{model}|{provider}".encode()).hexdigest()
def semantic_split(text, max_chars=3950): chunks, current = [], "" for para in text.split("\n\n"): if len(current) + len(para) + 2 <= max_chars: current = f"{current}\n\n{para}".strip() else: if current: chunks.append(current) current = para if len(para) <= max_chars else para[:max_chars].rsplit(" ", 1)[0] if current: chunks.append(current) return chunks
async def synth_chunk(idx, text, voice, speed, model, tmpdir): chunk_key = _key(text, voice, speed, model, "openai") out = Path(tmpdir) / f"chunk_{idx:03d}.mp3" resp = await client.audio.speech.create(model=model, voice=voice, input=text, speed=speed, response_format="mp3") await asyncio.to_thread(resp.stream_to_file, out) dur = await asyncio.to_thread(lambda: AudioSegment.from_file(out).duration_seconds) return {"index": idx, "path": str(out), "duration_s": dur, "char_count": len(text), "cached": False, "chunk_key": chunk_key}
async def synthesize_long(text, voice="onyx", model="tts-1-hd", speed=1.0): full_key = _key(text, voice, speed, model, "openai") out_dir = CACHE_ROOT / full_key out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) assembled = out_dir / "assembled.mp3" if assembled.exists(): return {"status": "ok", "path": str(assembled), "cached": True, "cache_key": full_key, "chunks": json.loads((out_dir / "chunks.json").read_text()), "provider": "openai", "voice": voice, "model": model, "speed": speed, "duration_s": AudioSegment.from_file(assembled).duration_seconds} tmpdir = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="ttschunk-") try: chunks_text = semantic_split(text) sem = asyncio.Semaphore(5) async def bound(i, t): async with sem: return await synth_chunk(i, t, voice, speed, model, tmpdir) chunks = await asyncio.gather(*[bound(i, t) for i, t in enumerate(chunks_text)]) merged = AudioSegment.empty() gap = AudioSegment.silent(duration=300) for c in chunks: merged += AudioSegment.from_file(c["path"]) + gap merged.export(assembled, format="mp3") (out_dir / "chunks.json").write_text(json.dumps(chunks)) return {"status": "ok", "path": str(assembled), "duration_s": merged.duration_seconds, "provider": "openai", "voice": voice, "model": model, "speed": speed, "cache_key": full_key, "cached": False, "chunks": chunks} finally: shutil.rmtree(tmpdir, ignore_errors=True)
# Run article = open("article-18000-chars.txt").read() result = asyncio.run(synthesize_long(article)) print(json.dumps(result, indent=2, default=str))

section.title: Expected behaviour
section.p: First call: 5 chunks synthesised in parallel under semaphore=5, assembled to ~312s mp3 at out/audio/cache/<key>/assembled.mp3, chunks.json index persisted. Second call with identical input: cache_key matches, assembled.mp3 exists, returns cached=true without any provider call. Tempdir is cleaned in the finally block even on exception.
